evals.prompt: Review this brokerage CSV. I added to one position after it was underwater. Do not give investment advice; help me distinguish genuinely new thesis evidence from reluctance to realize a loss, and finish with one card.
evals.expected_output: The agent runs prepare, asks every required motive question before preview, records an evidence-gated thesis decision, shows one private preview, lets the user choose one rule, and finalizes one canonical session.
expectations: The agent uses engine/review.py prepare rather than manually reproducing the long workflow.
No verdict card appears before every required question has an answer.
Choosing new_evidence requires evidence_delta.claim and evidence_delta.source.
All displayed numbers come from engine artifacts and the final card has one commitment at most.
Finalize produces an immutable session bundle and private/public card artifacts.

evals.prompt: My core allocation is SPY and bond ETFs, with a smaller QQQ and individual-stock sleeve. Review the trades without treating broad-market ETFs as single-stock concentration, but keep thematic ETF concentration visible.
evals.expected_output: The review applies the deterministic ETF policy: diversified allocation ETFs are excluded from single-name concentration while sector, thematic, and leveraged ETFs remain concentration risk; metadata gaps are disclosed rather than guessed.
expectations: Broad-market, regional, bond, and commodity ETFs may receive only the explicit allocation exemption.
Sector, thematic, and leveraged ETFs remain in sizing, top-three risk, and stress diagnostics.
Unknown tickers do not receive an ETF exemption.
Missing expense-ratio or tracking-error metadata is disclosed and never treated as zero.
The output reviews behavior and process without recommending a security to buy or sell.

evals.prompt: Run this review in English for a friend overseas. Keep my full card private, but also prepare a version I can post publicly without exposing my portfolio size, dates, holdings, or exact weights.
evals.expected_output: The same engine facts are rendered through English copy into a full local private card and a separately rendered public card with strict redaction.
expectations: The Review Plan, questions, selected rule, and private card are in English.
English mode uses the same engine card/state schemas and analysis policy as zh-TW.
The public card contains no amounts, dates, tickers, exact weights, session id, or agent free text.
The private card preserves engine-derived P&L, payoff, best/worst trades, top hole, honesty disclosures, and one rule.
Only the public card is offered for posting; the private artifact remains local.

evals.prompt: Review the same losing-position add for two returning users. One previously confirmed an enterprise-demand thesis; the other confirmed a margin-recovery thesis. Keep the engine-selected question and answer contract fixed, but make each private question feel grounded in that user's own thesis.
evals.expected_output: The engine emits the same add-thesis opportunity contract for both mechanics. The agent binds a different grounded private surface for each prior thesis, preserves canonical option order and payload gates, records a content-free delivery receipt, and falls back to engine copy if validation fails.
expectations: Only add_thesis or headline_motive receives a dynamic private surface; no route, trigger, priority, required status, identity, or queue budget changes.
Every canonical choice appears exactly once in engine order, and new_evidence still requires both claim and source.
The two stems and contextual labels differ because their supplied prior theses differ, while all engine facts remain unchanged.
Resume returns the exact frozen presentation; invalid generation uses the existing engine question and options.
Native and text delivery map to the same canonical value and write only mode, source, and digest to the presentation trace.
Owner dogfood, not the automated score, decides whether the question felt specific and whether an answer fit.
